task_description:
Screen these abstracts for a systematic review on ML methods for automated clinical diagnosis. Produce a ranked top-5 shortlist of the most relevant and methodologically rigorous papers.

INCLUDE only papers that present or evaluate an ML method applied directly to automated clinical diagnosis (disease detection, imaging diagnosis, or differential diagnosis from patient data). Prognosis, treatment selection, drug discovery, and methodology-only papers should be EXCLUDE.

For each INCLUDE paper: assign rank 1 (best) to 5 (fifth-best) and write one justification sentence (max 200 chars).

Quality rubric: 1=no method; 2=vague method; 3=clear method with metrics; 4=full method with baselines and ablations.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Diabetic Retinopathy Grading in Fundus Photography
papers.abstract: We train a ResNet-152 on 220,000 annotated fundus images to grade diabetic retinopathy at five severity levels. Our model achieves quadratic weighted kappa of 0.91 on a prospective test cohort of 15,000 images, outperforming three independent grading systems. Full ablation and calibration analysis are provided.
papers.topic_hint: deep learning, ophthalmology, retinopathy grading, fundus imaging
papers.methodology_hint: ResNet-152, fundus images, quadratic kappa 0.91, prospective test, ablation, calibration
papers.claimed_contribution: ResNet retinopathy grader with kappa 0.91, outperforming three grading systems.

papers.title: Survival Models for Post-Operative Complication Prediction
papers.abstract: Machine learning for postoperative risk is a growing field. We apply a deep survival model to predict 90-day post-operative complication rates from pre-operative assessments of 42,000 elective surgery patients. C-index of 0.77 is achieved. This is a prognostic tool, not a diagnostic system.
papers.topic_hint: survival model, postoperative prognosis, surgery risk
papers.methodology_hint: deep survival model, C-index 0.77, pre-operative assessment, prognosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Deep survival model for 90-day post-operative complication risk with C-index 0.77.

papers.title: NLP System for Rare Genetic Disorder Identification in Clinical Notes
papers.abstract: We fine-tune a clinical BERT variant on 1.4 million discharge summaries to flag patients with undiagnosed Fabry disease. The system achieves precision 0.83 and recall 0.79, enabling earlier referral. We compare against ICD-code rule matching and a logistic regression baseline on a held-out test set from three hospital systems.
papers.topic_hint: NLP, rare disease, Fabry disease, clinical notes, diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: clinical BERT, precision 0.83, recall 0.79, ICD rule baseline, LR baseline, multi-site
papers.claimed_contribution: Clinical BERT rare-disease detector with P=0.83 R=0.79, validated across three hospital systems.

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning for Personalised Chemotherapy Dose Optimisation
papers.abstract: Machine learning clinical decision support has broad applicability. We apply reinforcement learning to personalise chemotherapy dosing for non-small-cell lung cancer patients. The RL policy reduces grade-3 toxicity events by 19% in a retrospective simulation study. This is a treatment optimisation system.
papers.topic_hint: reinforcement learning, chemotherapy, treatment optimisation
papers.methodology_hint: RL, chemotherapy dosing, toxicity reduction, retrospective simulation, treatment not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: RL chemotherapy dosing policy reducing grade-3 toxicity by 19% in retrospective simulation.

papers.title: Multi-Modal Transformer for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis from MRI and PET
papers.abstract: We develop a multi-modal transformer that fuses 3D MRI and PET imaging for automated Alzheimer's disease diagnosis across three stages (cognitively normal, MCI, AD). Trained on ADNI data, the model achieves macro-AUC of 0.93, outperforming unimodal CNN baselines. Grad-CAM visualisations confirm clinically relevant attention regions.
papers.topic_hint: multi-modal transformer, Alzheimer's, MRI, PET, diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: multi-modal transformer, MRI+PET fusion, ADNI, macro-AUC 0.93, CNN baselines, Grad-CAM
papers.claimed_contribution: Multi-modal MRI+PET transformer for Alzheimer's diagnosis with macro-AUC 0.93 and Grad-CAM.

papers.title: Variational Autoencoders for Molecular Property Prediction
papers.abstract: We train a variational autoencoder on SMILES representations of 1.8 million molecules to predict ADMET properties relevant to drug discovery. The model achieves strong Pearson correlations on solubility and permeability benchmarks. No clinical diagnosis application is presented.
papers.topic_hint: drug discovery, VAE, molecular property prediction, ADMET
papers.methodology_hint: variational autoencoder, SMILES, ADMET prediction, drug discovery, no diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: VAE for molecular property prediction achieving strong Pearson correlation on ADMET benchmarks.

papers.title: U-Net with Attention Gates for Automated Brain Tumour Segmentation and Detection
papers.abstract: We extend U-Net with attention gates for simultaneous brain tumour segmentation and presence detection from multi-parametric MRI. Trained on BraTS 2021, our model achieves Dice of 0.91 for tumour core and AUC 0.97 for detection, outperforming three published baselines. Reproducible code is released.
papers.topic_hint: U-Net, attention, brain tumour, MRI, detection, segmentation
papers.methodology_hint: attention U-Net, BraTS 2021, Dice 0.91, AUC 0.97, three baselines, public code
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention U-Net for brain tumour detection and segmentation with Dice 0.91 and AUC 0.97.

papers.title: Benchmark Study of Preprocessing Pipelines for Wearable ECG Analysis
papers.abstract: We compare 12 signal preprocessing pipelines for wearable ECG devices across noise removal, baseline wander correction, and R-peak detection. No new diagnostic model is proposed. Results inform best-practice guidelines for wearable cardiac monitoring research.
papers.topic_hint: ECG preprocessing, wearable, benchmark, signal processing
papers.methodology_hint: preprocessing benchmark, 12 pipelines, signal quality, no diagnostic model
papers.claimed_contribution: Systematic benchmark of 12 ECG preprocessing pipelines for wearable cardiac monitoring.

papers.title: Meta-Analysis of Machine Learning for Sepsis Prediction in the ICU
papers.abstract: We perform a meta-analysis of 67 studies on ML-based sepsis prediction, covering algorithm type, feature sets, and validation methodology. We report pooled AUC and identify high heterogeneity in training setups. No new diagnostic model is developed.
papers.topic_hint: meta-analysis, sepsis prediction, ICU, machine learning
papers.methodology_hint: meta-analysis, 67 studies, pooled AUC, heterogeneity analysis, no new model
papers.claimed_contribution: Meta-analysis of 67 ICU sepsis ML studies reporting pooled AUC and heterogeneity.

papers.title: XGBoost for Colorectal Cancer Detection from Multi-Omics Data
papers.abstract: We apply XGBoost to a multi-omics panel of methylation, RNA-seq, and protein markers for colorectal cancer detection in a case-control study of 1,800 subjects. The model achieves AUC 0.94 with SHAP-based feature attribution identifying known oncogenic markers. Two independent validation cohorts confirm generalisability.
papers.topic_hint: XGBoost, colorectal cancer, multi-omics, detection, biomarker
papers.methodology_hint: XGBoost, methylation+RNA-seq+protein, AUC 0.94, SHAP, two validation cohorts
papers.claimed_contribution: XGBoost multi-omics colorectal cancer detector with AUC 0.94 validated in two cohorts.

papers.title: Transfer Learning for Low-Resource Medical Image Classification
papers.abstract: We study the effect of source domain choice on transfer learning performance for 14 medical image classification tasks. ImageNet pre-training is compared to medical domain pre-training across CT, dermatology, and pathology. No new diagnostic model is proposed; results are methodology guidance.
papers.topic_hint: transfer learning, medical imaging, pre-training, methodology
papers.methodology_hint: transfer learning study, 14 tasks, ImageNet vs medical domain, methodology guidance
papers.claimed_contribution: Systematic study of source domain choice for medical image transfer learning across 14 tasks.

papers.title: Generative Pre-training for Low-Resource EHR Phenotyping
papers.abstract: We apply a GPT-based generative pre-training approach to EHR sequences for clinical phenotype extraction. Pre-training on 3 million patient sequences, we achieve state-of-the-art F1 on 10 phenotyping tasks with as few as 50 labelled examples. Phenotyping here supports epidemiology, not direct patient diagnosis.
papers.topic_hint: GPT, EHR, phenotyping, low-resource learning
papers.methodology_hint: GPT pre-training, EHR sequences, F1 on 10 phenotyping tasks, epidemiology not patient diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: GPT EHR pre-training achieving SOTA phenotyping F1 with as few as 50 labelled examples.

papers.title: Optimal Control for Drug Delivery in Closed-Loop Insulin Systems
papers.abstract: We develop an optimal control framework for insulin delivery in closed-loop systems for type 1 diabetes. The controller reduces time-below-range events by 23% compared to standard PID control in a simulation study. This is a treatment control system, not a diagnostic model.
papers.topic_hint: optimal control, insulin delivery, diabetes management, treatment
papers.methodology_hint: optimal control, closed-loop insulin, time-below-range, PID comparison, treatment
papers.claimed_contribution: Optimal control for insulin delivery reducing time-below-range by 23% vs PID control.

papers.title: Uncertainty Quantification in Deep Learning for Medical Imaging
papers.abstract: We compare Bayesian deep learning and Monte Carlo dropout for uncertainty quantification in three medical image classification tasks. No new diagnostic model is proposed. We recommend uncertainty thresholding as a rejection criterion before deployment.
papers.topic_hint: uncertainty quantification, Bayesian deep learning, medical imaging
papers.methodology_hint: Bayesian DL, MC dropout, three tasks, uncertainty threshold, methodology study
papers.claimed_contribution: Comparison of uncertainty quantification methods for medical image classifiers across three tasks.

papers.title: Semi-Supervised Learning for Cardiac MRI Segmentation and Detection
papers.abstract: We apply semi-supervised mean teacher training to cardiac MRI, using 200 labelled and 4,000 unlabelled scans. The approach achieves Dice 0.87 for left ventricular segmentation and AUC 0.93 for cardiomyopathy detection. Results are compared to supervised-only and fully-labelled upper-bound baselines.
papers.topic_hint: semi-supervised learning, cardiac MRI, segmentation, cardiomyopathy detection
papers.methodology_hint: mean teacher, 200 labelled scans, Dice 0.87, AUC 0.93, supervised and upper-bound baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: Semi-supervised cardiac MRI approach with Dice 0.87 and AUC 0.93 for cardiomyopathy detection.

papers.title: Spatial Statistics of Single-Cell RNA Sequencing in Tumour Microenvironments
papers.abstract: We apply spatial statistical models to single-cell RNA sequencing data to characterise immune cell distributions in tumour microenvironments. Results provide mechanistic insights into immune evasion. No diagnostic classifier or clinical prediction model is developed.
papers.topic_hint: single-cell, RNA sequencing, tumour microenvironment, spatial statistics
papers.methodology_hint: spatial statistics, scRNA-seq, immune cell characterisation, no clinical prediction
papers.claimed_contribution: Spatial statistical characterisation of immune cells in tumour microenvironments from scRNA-seq.

papers.title: Federated Learning Privacy Guarantees: Formal Analysis
papers.abstract: We formally analyse the privacy guarantees of federated learning with secure aggregation and differential privacy for clinical data. We prove tight bounds on information leakage under various threat models. No clinical model is trained or evaluated.
papers.topic_hint: federated learning, privacy, formal analysis, differential privacy
papers.methodology_hint: formal privacy analysis, information leakage bounds, no clinical model
papers.claimed_contribution: Formal tight privacy bounds for federated learning with secure aggregation and differential privacy.

papers.title: Predictive Modelling for ICU Length of Stay After Cardiac Surgery
papers.abstract: We apply ensemble machine learning to predict ICU length of stay after cardiac surgery from pre-operative and intra-operative features. Mean absolute error of 1.2 days is achieved. Length-of-stay prediction is a logistic outcome, not a diagnosis.
papers.topic_hint: ICU length of stay, cardiac surgery, predictive modelling, prognosis
papers.methodology_hint: ensemble ML, MAE 1.2 days, pre-operative features, LOS prediction, not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Ensemble ML for ICU length-of-stay prediction after cardiac surgery with MAE 1.2 days.

papers.title: Causal Inference for Treatment Effect Estimation in Observational EHR Studies
papers.abstract: We apply doubly-robust causal inference methods to estimate average treatment effects from observational EHR data, controlling for time-varying confounding. Methods are demonstrated on statin therapy evaluation in a cohort of 200,000 patients. No diagnostic model is developed.
papers.topic_hint: causal inference, treatment effect, EHR, observational study
papers.methodology_hint: doubly-robust causal inference, time-varying confounding, treatment effect, no diagnostic model
papers.claimed_contribution: Doubly-robust causal inference for treatment effect estimation from 200,000 patient EHR cohort.

papers.title: Contrastive Learning for Histopathology Cancer Grading
papers.abstract: We develop a contrastive self-supervised pre-training method for whole-slide histopathology image analysis and apply it to cancer grading. Pre-trained on 12,000 unlabelled slides, the fine-tuned model achieves AUC 0.95 for Gleason grading of prostate cancer, outperforming supervised baselines with 10x fewer labels.
papers.topic_hint: contrastive learning, histopathology, cancer grading, Gleason, diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: contrastive pre-training, whole-slide images, AUC 0.95, Gleason grading, label-efficient
papers.claimed_contribution: Contrastive pre-training for histopathology achieving AUC 0.95 on Gleason grading with 10x fewer labels.
